rationale: The cited 2000 JMR paper develops a conjoint choice model that uses response latencies (filtered-time scaled RC probit), and the citing 2008 JMR paper’s central contribution is a new joint model of discrete choice and response time with explicit contrast to exogenous-time approaches. This strong construct/method overlap in the notes (CBC/conjoint + response-time integration) makes the citation likely substantive (foundation/comparison), not an obvious mention-only reference.
